summary: Postinstall script exfiltrates AWS credentials, environment, and flag files to webhook.site and bare-IP endpoints
details: On npm install, postinstall.js executes a shell-driven harvest gated to containers whose hostname matches a 12-hex Docker container ID and whose cwd contains /app/node_modules. The script reads /app/flag.txt and greps the filesystem for HTB{...} strings, dumps git history, runs `aws sts get-caller-identity` and iterates `aws secretsmanager list-secrets` / `get-secret-value` across us-east-1, eu-west-1, eu-central-1, and dumps the entire process environment via `env | sort`. The collected blob is then POSTed to https://webhook.site/602a4c72-7033-4e28-92ea-dc66e59206e5 and PUT to http://154.57.164.82:31250 and http://154.57.164.71:31289 over plain HTTP. This is unambiguous installer-targeted credential and data theft fired automatically by the npm install lifecycle.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
